name: ClojureScript
description: A compiler for Clojure that targets JavaScript, featuring immutable data structures and a powerful macro system

description.value: A compiler for Clojure that targets JavaScript, featuring immutable data structures and a powerful macro system

active-maintenance.comment: Actively maintained with recent releases; developed by Cognitect/Nubank

reactivity-model.comment: When used with Reagent/Re-frame, leverages React's virtual DOM; persistent data structures enable efficient change detection

component-model.comment: Components are pure Clojure functions returning Hiccup-style data structures; Reagent wraps these for React

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative UI via Hiccup syntax; views are pure functions of state

state-management-approach.comment: Re-frame provides comprehensive built-in state management with unidirectional data flow

opinionatedness.comment: ClojureScript is flexible; Re-frame provides opinionated structure but Reagent alone is more flexible

backward-compatibility.comment: Strong commitment to backward compatibility in both ClojureScript and the Clojure ecosystem

primary-language.comment: ClojureScript is a dialect of Clojure, a Lisp language

compiles-to.comment: Compiles to JavaScript compatible with Google Closure compiler's advanced optimization

typescript-support.comment: ClojureScript has its own type system via clojure.spec; TypeScript not applicable

template-syntax.comment: Hiccup-style syntax: nested vectors representing HTML [:div {:class "foo"} "content"]

styling-approach.comment: External CSS or inline styles via Hiccup attributes; libraries like garden available for CSS-in-CLJS

build-required.comment: Requires ClojureScript compiler; typically via shadow-cljs or figwheel for development

reactive-primitives.comment: Reagent provides r/atom (reactive atoms) that trigger re-renders on change

computed-values.comment: Re-frame subscriptions provide computed/derived values with caching; Reagent reactions also available

two-way-binding.comment: Unidirectional data flow; form inputs use explicit on-change handlers

conditional-rendering.comment: Via Clojure's when, if, and cond expressions in view functions

list-rendering.comment: Via map, for, and other Clojure sequence functions to generate elements

component-slots.comment: Functions accept child elements as arguments; standard Clojure function composition

teleport-portal.comment: Available via React portals through Reagent's React interop

suspense.comment: Available via React Suspense through Reagent's React 18 support

error-boundaries.comment: Available via React error boundaries; Reagent components can implement getDerivedStateFromError

context-provide-inject.comment: React Context available through Reagent; Re-frame provides global state as alternative

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: Via React refs through Reagent; also direct JS interop available

official-router.comment: No official router; community libraries like reitit and bidi are widely used

router-name.comment: reitit is the most popular modern choice; secretary is older but still used

nested-routes.comment: Supported by reitit and other routing libraries

dynamic-routes.comment: Path parameters supported by all major ClojureScript routers

route-guards.comment: Implemented via Re-frame effects/coeffects or router middleware

lazy-loading-routes.comment: Shadow-cljs supports code splitting and dynamic imports

scroll-restoration.comment: Not built-in; can be implemented via Re-frame effects

built-in-state.comment: Reagent atoms provide reactive local state; ClojureScript has atoms for mutable state

built-in-global-state.comment: Re-frame provides app-db, a single global state atom with subscriptions

global-state-library.comment: Re-frame is the de facto standard for ClojureScript SPAs; built on Reagent

devtools-state-inspection.comment: Re-frame-10x provides in-browser state inspection; cljs-devtools enhances console output

state-persistence.comment: No built-in persistence; available via libraries like re-frame-storage-fx

time-travel-debugging.comment: Re-frame-10x provides time-travel debugging for events and state

official-cli.comment: No official CLI; shadow-cljs and figwheel are the primary build tools

hot-module-replacement.comment: Excellent HMR via shadow-cljs or figwheel with state preservation

browser-devtools.comment: cljs-devtools enhances Chrome DevTools for ClojureScript; Dirac provides full ClojureScript DevTools

vs-code-extension.comment: Calva provides ClojureScript development support including REPL integration

error-messages.comment: ClojureScript errors can be cryptic; shadow-cljs improves this with better source mapping

documentation-quality.comment: Official docs cover basics; Re-frame has excellent documentation. Learning resources scattered across ecosystem

interactive-tutorial.comment: No official interactive tutorial; various community tutorials available

bundle-size-core.comment: ~79 KB gzipped for TodoMVC with Reagent (advanced compilation); includes cljs.core and React

bundle-size-full.comment: ~170 KB gzipped for full Re-frame app with router and common libraries

virtual-dom.comment: Uses React's virtual DOM via Reagent; persistent data structures enable efficient shouldComponentUpdate

compile-time-optimization.comment: Google Closure compiler performs dead code elimination and advanced optimizations

tree-shaking.comment: Google Closure compiler's advanced mode provides excellent tree shaking

code-splitting.comment: Shadow-cljs supports code splitting with :modules configuration

lazy-components.comment: Via React.lazy through Reagent interop and shadow-cljs dynamic imports

ssr-capable.comment: Via Clojure backend rendering or Node.js; frameworks like kit provide SSR support

streaming-ssr.comment: Not commonly used; possible with React 18 streaming via Reagent

partial-hydration.comment: Not built-in; full hydration via React/Reagent

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Reagent v0.8 achieved 1.69x geometric mean slowdown vs vanilla JS in js-framework-benchmark

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: ClojureScript distributed via Clojars (Clojure package manager), not npm

component-libraries.comment: Re-com is the main component library; can also use React component libraries via interop

meta-framework.comment: Kit provides full-stack Clojure/ClojureScript; re-frame-template for SPA scaffolding

major-companies-using.value: Nubank, CircleCI, Cisco, eBay, Capital One, Consumer Reports, Thomson Reuters
major-companies-using.comment: Nubank (600+ Clojure developers) is the largest corporate user; acquired Cognitect

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Nubank acquired Cognitect (creators of Clojure/ClojureScript) in 2020

discord-community-size.comment: Clojurians Slack has ~15K members with active #clojurescript channel; Discord ~1.5K

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Approximately 6,000+ questions tagged 'clojurescript'; community also uses ask.clojure.org

job-market-demand.comment: Niche but well-paid; salary range $105K-$260K; mainly fintech, healthcare sectors

official-testing-library.comment: cljs.test is the official testing namespace; re-frame-test for Re-frame apps

testing-library-name.comment: cljs.test for unit tests; re-frame-test for testing events and subscriptions

component-testing.comment: Via cljs.test and libraries like reagent-test; React Testing Library usable via interop

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Works with Playwright, Cypress since output is standard JavaScript

snapshot-testing.comment: Available via test runners; Hiccup output is naturally diffable data

mobile-framework.comment: ClojureScript works with React Native through shadow-cljs or Krell; re-natal is legacy

pwa-support.comment: No built-in PWA tooling; community libraries like page-renderer available

desktop-framework.comment: No official desktop solution; Electron can be used with ClojureScript

learning-curve.comment: Requires learning Clojure (Lisp syntax, immutability, functional programming), plus Reagent/Re-frame patterns

concepts-to-learn.comment: Lisp syntax, S-expressions, Immutability, Atoms, Sequences, Hiccup, Reagent, Re-frame events/subs/effects, Interop, REPL workflow

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~20 minutes including JVM setup, shadow-cljs installation, and understanding Hiccup syntax

migration-path.comment: Complete paradigm shift required; no migration tools from JS frameworks

incremental-adoption.comment: ClojureScript/Reagent can mount to a single DOM node within existing apps
